title: Add race condition detection to Go tests
description:
# GitHub Issue #145: Add race condition detection to Go tests with -race flag

**Category**: testing / reliability | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Unit and integration tests currently run without the `-race` flag. Kubernetes operator code is inherently concurrent — reconcilers, watches, and informer caches all operate across goroutines. Race conditions are a frequent and hard-to-diagnose source of bugs in this type of codebase. A dedicated CI job should run all Go tests with `-race` enabled, and a corresponding Makefile target should be added for local use.

Makefile extension:

```makefile
test-race:
	cd internal/common && go test -race ./...
	cd operators/keystone && go test -race ./...
```

Proposed CI job (separate from the main test job since `-race` adds ~2-5x overhead):

```yaml
test-race:
  needs: [changes]
  if: needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 20
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
      with:
        go-version-file: go.work
    - run: |
        cd internal/common && go test -race -count=1 ./...
        cd operators/keystone && go test -race -count=1 ./...
```

**Motivation**: The Go race detector is the standard tool for catching data races at test time. Without it, race conditions can silently corrupt shared state in production — particularly dangerous in operator code where concurrent reconcile loops may read and write the same resources. Running `-race` as a separate job avoids slowing down the primary test feedback loop while still catching races on every PR.

**Affected Areas**:
- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
- `Makefile`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] New `test-race` Makefile target runs `go test -race ./...` for all Go modules
- [ ] New `test-race` CI job in `ci.yaml` gated on `needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`
- [ ] CI job uses `-count=1` to disable test caching (race results must not be cached)
- [ ] CI job has a 20-minute timeout to accommodate the overhead of the race detector
- [ ] Job fails the pipeline when a data race is detected
- [ ] Existing test jobs are not modified

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/145

stories.title: Developer detects race conditions locally via Makefile
stories.want: to run race-detected Go tests locally with a single `make test-race` command
stories.so_that: I can find data races in operator code before pushing to CI
criteria: `make test-race` runs `go test -race ./...` for internal/common module
`make test-race` runs `go test -race ./...` for each operator module (keystone, c5c3)
Target uses the OPERATORS variable so new operators are included automatically
Non-zero exit code when any module's tests detect a data race

stories.title: CI automatically detects data races on every PR
stories.want: a dedicated CI job to run all Go tests with the race detector on every PR that touches Go code
stories.so_that: race conditions are caught before merging into main
criteria: Job is skipped when only non-Go files change (docs, helm, etc.)
Job uses `-count=1` to prevent stale cached results masking races
Job has a 20-minute timeout to accommodate race detector overhead
Workflow run is marked as failed when a data race is detected

stories.title: Existing CI pipeline is not disrupted by race detection
stories.want: the new race detection job to not modify or slow down existing test jobs
stories.so_that: the primary test feedback loop remains fast and unchanged
criteria: `test` job YAML block is byte-identical before and after the change
`test-integration` job YAML block is byte-identical before and after
No new `needs:` entries are added to existing jobs (e2e-operator, tempest, etc.)
`test-race` runs independently — not on the critical path for E2E or publish jobs

requirements.description: The Makefile SHALL include a `test-race` phony target that runs `go test -race ./...` for internal/common and every operator module
requirements.rationale: Developers need a single command to run race-detected tests locally before pushing

scenarios.name: All modules tested with race detector
scenarios.then: `go test -race` executes for internal/common, operators/keystone, and operators/c5c3
and_then: exit code 0 if no races detected in any module

scenarios.name: Single operator override
scenarios.then: `go test -race` executes for internal/common and operators/keystone only

scenarios.name: Race detected causes failure
scenarios.when: a test triggers a data race in any module
and_then: race detector output is printed to stderr

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL include a `test-race` job that is gated on Go code changes via `needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`
requirements.rationale: Race detection should only run when Go code changes, avoiding wasted CI minutes on docs/helm changes

scenarios.name: Job runs on Go changes
scenarios.when: a PR modifies files in internal/, operators/, or go.work
and_then: job appears in the PR checks list

scenarios.name: Job skipped on non-Go changes
scenarios.when: a PR only modifies files in docs/ or operators/*/helm/
and_then: job shows as skipped in the PR checks, not failed

scenarios.name: Job runs on tag pushes
scenarios.when: a v* tag is pushed (triggering full release pipeline)
scenarios.then: the `test-race` job runs because ci-resolve-changes.sh forces `go=true`

requirements.description: The CI `test-race` job SHALL use the `-count=1` flag to disable test result caching
requirements.rationale: Race conditions are non-deterministic; cached passing results could mask a real race on subsequent runs

scenarios.name: Tests run fresh every time
and_then: no `(cached)` markers appear in test output

scenarios.name: Local Makefile does not force -count=1
scenarios.then: go test caching behavior is the default (no -count=1)
and_then: developers can benefit from caching during iterative local development

requirements.rationale: The race detector adds 2-5x overhead; 20 minutes provides headroom over the 10-minute base test timeout

scenarios.name: Job completes within timeout
scenarios.when: race-detected tests run with normal test suite

scenarios.name: Hung test is terminated
scenarios.when: a test hangs due to a deadlock exposed by the race detector
scenarios.then: GitHub Actions terminates the job at the 20-minute mark
and_then: the job is marked as failed due to timeout

requirements.description: The CI workflow SHALL fail when the `test-race` job detects a data race
requirements.rationale: Race conditions must block merging — they indicate potential data corruption in production

scenarios.name: Race detected fails the workflow
scenarios.when: go test -race detects a data race during CI
scenarios.then: the `test-race` job exits with non-zero status
and_then: the overall workflow run is marked as failed

scenarios.name: Clean run passes the workflow
scenarios.when: go test -race completes without detecting any races
and_then: the job shows a green check in the PR

requirements.description: Existing CI test jobs (test, test-integration) SHALL NOT be modified
requirements.rationale: The race detector adds significant overhead; existing fast-feedback test jobs must remain unchanged

scenarios.name: test job unchanged
scenarios.when: the CI workflow is reviewed after adding test-race
scenarios.then: the `test` job definition is identical to the version before the change

scenarios.name: test-integration job unchanged
scenarios.when: the CI workflow is reviewed after adding test-race
scenarios.then: the `test-integration` job definition is identical to the version before the change

scenarios.name: No new dependencies on test-race
scenarios.when: the CI workflow is reviewed after adding test-race
scenarios.then: no existing job lists `test-race` in its `needs:` array
and_then: test-race runs independently and does not block E2E or publish jobs

tasks.title: Add `test-race` phony target to Makefile in the Test Targets section (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Add a `.PHONY: test-race` target after the existing `test-operator` target in the Test Targets section. The target runs `go test -race` using workspace-relative paths for internal/common and iterates over $(OPERATORS) for operator modules, matching the pattern of the existing `test` target. Include a comment referencing CC-0052. Do NOT include `-count=1` (that is CI-specific). Verify with `make test-race` locally.

tasks.title: Add `test-race` CI job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` (REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006)
tasks.description: Add a new `test-race` job after the `test-integration` job block in ci.yaml. The job needs [changes], is gated on `needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`, uses ubuntu-latest, and has timeout-minutes: 20. Steps: (1) actions/checkout with same SHA pin as other jobs, (2) actions/setup-go with same SHA pin and go-version-file: go.work, (3) run step that executes `go test -race -count=1` for all three modules (internal/common, operators/keystone, operators/c5c3) using workspace-relative paths. Include a CC-0052 comment. Do NOT modify any existing job. Do NOT add test-race to any existing job's `needs:` array.

tasks.title: Update `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` with test-race job documentation (REQ-002)
tasks.description: Update the CI workflow reference doc: (1) Add `test-race` to the Job Dependency DAG ASCII art in the Gate Jobs section. (2) Add a `### test-race` section after the `### test-integration` section documenting the job's purpose (CC-0052), step table, timeout, and -count=1 rationale. Follow the existing section format (description line, step table with Action/Details columns). Reference CC-0052 in the section header comment.

test_specifications.story: Developer detects race conditions locally via Makefile
test_specifications.expected: `make test-race` should execute `go test -race` for internal/common, operators/keystone, and operators/c5c3, exiting 0 when no races are detected

test_specifications.story: Developer detects race conditions locally via Makefile
test_specifications.expected: `make test-race OPERATOR=keystone` should run race tests for internal/common and operators/keystone only, skipping c5c3

test_specifications.story: CI automatically detects data races on every PR
test_specifications.expected: Job has `needs: [changes]` and `if: needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'` — skipped when only non-Go files change

test_specifications.story: CI automatically detects data races on every PR
test_specifications.expected: Job uses same SHA-pinned checkout and setup-go actions as other jobs (de0fac2e..., 4b73464b...)

test_specifications.story: CI automatically detects data races on every PR
test_specifications.expected: Run step includes `-count=1` in all `go test` invocations to prevent cached results

test_specifications.story: CI automatically detects data races on every PR
CI automatically detects data races on every PR
test_specifications.expected: No `continue-on-error` or `if: always()` on the run step — non-zero exit from go test -race fails the job

test_specifications.story: Existing CI pipeline is not disrupted by race detection
test_specifications.expected: git diff of ci.yaml shows only additions (new test-race job block) — no modifications to existing job blocks

test_specifications.story: Existing CI pipeline is not disrupted by race detection
test_specifications.expected: grep for 'test-race' in other jobs' `needs:` arrays returns no matches

review_criteria: `make test-race` exits 0 locally, running `go test -race` for all 3 modules (internal/common, operators/keystone, operators/c5c3)
CI `test-race` job uses SHA-pinned actions matching existing pins in ci.yaml (checkout@de0fac2e..., setup-go@4b73464b...)
CI run step includes `-race -count=1` for all 3 Go modules and does NOT include `-coverprofile` (race detection, not coverage)
CI job has `timeout-minutes: 20` and `needs: [changes]` with `if: needs.changes.outputs.go == 'true'`
No existing CI job is modified — diff shows only the new job block and no changes to existing blocks
No existing job's `needs:` array references `test-race` — the job runs independently
`docs/reference/ci-workflow.md` updated with test-race in the DAG and a new ### test-race section matching existing doc format
Makefile `test-race` target follows the OPERATORS iteration pattern of the existing `test` target (not hardcoded module list)
implementation_notes:
This is a small, additive feature touching 3 files. No architectural decisions needed.

**Makefile pattern**: Follow the existing `test` target pattern — workspace-relative paths (`./internal/common/...`, `./operators/$$op/...`), iterate over $(OPERATORS), echo module name before each run. Place the target after `test-operator` in the Test Targets section. Do NOT include `-count=1` in the Makefile (that's CI-specific per the acceptance criteria).

**CI job placement**: Insert after the `test-integration` job block (line ~195 in ci.yaml) and before `verify-codegen`. The job is a gate-level job but does NOT block E2E jobs — it runs independently. This is intentional: race detection overhead should not sit on the critical path for E2E execution.

**CI action pins**: All actions must use the exact same SHA pins as other jobs in the file:
- `actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6`
- `actions/setup-go@4b73464bb391d4059bd26b0524d20df3927bd417 # v6`

**CI run step**: Use workspace-relative paths (not `cd`) to match the `test` / `test-integration` job patterns. Three separate `go test` invocations for clear per-module output:
```
go test -race -count=1 ./internal/common/...
go test -race -count=1 ./operators/keystone/...
go test -race -count=1 ./operators/c5c3/...
```

**Why not reuse `make test-race` in CI**: The Makefile target intentionally omits `-count=1` (for developer convenience). CI needs `-count=1` to prevent cached results masking non-deterministic races. Rather than adding complexity (env vars, extra targets), the CI step runs the commands directly.

**Docs update**: The ci-workflow.md doc has a specific format for job sections (description, step table, details). Follow it exactly. Update the ASCII DAG to include test-race as a gate job.

**Pitfalls**:
- Do NOT use `cd` in the CI run step without subshells — sequential `cd` commands in a bash script change the working directory permanently, causing subsequent relative paths to fail.
- Do NOT add `test-race` to any existing job's `needs:` array — the issue explicitly requires existing jobs to remain unmodified.
- The race detector requires CGO; ubuntu-latest runners have GCC pre-installed, so no additional setup is needed.
